programs.name: North Carolina Homestead Exemption
programs.what_it_does: This property tax relief program reduces the annual tax burden for qualifying seniors by exempting a portion of their home's assessed value from property taxes. It provides financial relief to help low-income elderly homeowners afford housing costs.
programs.who_its_for: Residents aged 65 or older who meet county-specific income thresholds and own and occupy their primary residence.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific property tax exemption tailored to NC seniors with varying income limits by county, beyond standard federal benefits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by county and year; reduces taxable value of primary residence (e.g., up to $38,000 in some areas as of recent years)[1]

programs.name: North Carolina Long-Term Care Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Covers skilled nursing facility care and some home-based long-term services under different eligibility rules than standard Medicaid. It helps pay for care costing $10,000-$15,000 monthly, preserving assets through planning.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors requiring nursing home level care, with income/assets qualifying under LTC-specific rules (often middle-income eligible via planning).
programs.whats_unique: NC state-administered Medicaid waiver program with asset protection strategies unique to state elder law practices, distinct from federal baseline.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of nursing home costs (up to $10k-$15k/month); asset limits ~$2,000 individual[1]

programs.name: Project C.A.R.E.
programs.what_it_does: Provides state-funded financial assistance for in-home or facility-based long-term care to eligible disabled veterans. Monthly payments help cover care expenses when medical costs exceed income.
programs.who_its_for: Honorably discharged veterans with 90 days active duty (including wartime), whose medical expenses exceed income.
programs.whats_unique: NC state-funded veteran aid program supplementing federal VA benefits, offering direct cash assistance for care not universally available.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $3,500 per month for care costs[1][4]

programs.name: North Carolina Seniors Farmers' Market Nutrition Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers vouchers to low-income seniors for purchasing fresh produce at farmers' markets and roadside stands. Promotes nutrition and supports local agriculture through state-funded coupons.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific nutrition voucher program unique to NC, not a federal standard benefit.
programs.benefit_value: Vouchers worth $50 annually per participant (varies by funding)[4]

programs.name: NC Lifespan Respite Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides planned or emergency relief to family caregivers of older adults or disabled individuals, offering respite services. Supports caregiver well-being through state-coordinated breaks from caregiving duties.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors or adults with disabilities.
programs.whats_unique: Statewide NC program focused on respite care coordination, beyond federal caregiver supports.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; respite services, training, and support (funding-dependent)[4]

programs.name: State-County Special Assistance - In Home
programs.what_it_does: Delivers state-county funded cash assistance for in-home care services to low-income frail seniors. Enables aging in place through direct support for personal care needs.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income older adults and disabled individuals needing in-home assistance.
programs.whats_unique: NC's state-county partnership program for in-home aid, distinct from federal SSI or Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Monthly cash payments scaled to need and income (e.g., up to state maximum ~$500+)[4]
